title: Roberto Waldteufel
text: Home * People * Roberto Waldteufel Roberto Waldteufel [1] Roberto Waldteufel , (born 13 Dec 1962) a French/British [2] computer games programmer born in Spain [3] , living on the Island of Jura, Scotland . Roberto is author of the Checkers program Wyllie [4] , and the chess program Rabbit [5] . Roberto Waldteufel seemed the first one, who mentioned the optimization trick in 1998 to dense the Rotated Bitboards tables, masking off the redundant outer occupancies [6] .
